core_mechanism: geometric-phase (Berry curvature) transport: a curvature in an internal parameter/momentum space acts as an effective monopole gauge field that adds an anomalous transverse (Magnus/Hall-type) velocity perpendicular to an applied driving force in the semiclassical equations of motion of a localized packet carrying an internal winding number
brief_reason: The abstract states that a magnetic-monopole Berry curvature appears in momentum space and produces a spin-orbit-type coupling and a Berry/Magnus transverse force on the wave packet, yielding a Hall effect — i.e. curvature-induced anomalous transverse deflection.
